title: Ambient Noise Level Monitoring and Trend Analysis (Meraki MT)
spl:
index=cisco_network sourcetype="meraki:api" sensor_type="noise" noise_db=*
| stats avg(noise_db) as avg_noise, max(noise_db) as peak_noise by location
| timechart avg(noise_db) by location
description: Tracks noise levels to ensure comfortable working environment and detect anomalies.
value: Tracks noise levels to ensure comfortable working environment and detect anomalies.
implementation: Ingest noise sensor data. Track by location and time of day.
visualization: Noise level gauge; time-of-day heat map; location comparison chart.
knownFalsePositives: Benign events during building tests, after-hours schedules, and vendor work—confirm against the BMS console before urgent response.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: `Cisco Meraki Add-on for Splunk` (Splunkbase 5580).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `sourcetype=meraki:api sensor_type="noise"`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Ingest noise sensor data. Track by location and time of day.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=cisco_network sourcetype="meraki:api" sensor_type="noise" noise_db=*
| stats avg(noise_db) as avg_noise, max(noise_db) as peak_noise by location
| timechart avg(noise_db) by location
```

Understanding this SPL

**Ambient Noise Level Monitoring and Trend Analysis (Meraki MT)** — Tracks noise levels to ensure comfortable working environment and detect anomalies.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype=meraki:api sensor_type="noise"`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `Cisco Meraki Add-on for Splunk` (Splunkbase 5580). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: cisco_network; **sourcetype**: meraki:api. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=cisco_network, sourcetype="meraki:api". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by location** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• `timechart` plots the metric over time with a separate series **by location** — ideal for trending and alerting on this use case.


Step 3 — Validate
Run the search in a known window. Spot-check a few events against the BMS console (for example Niagara, Tridium, or Honeywell) and confirm zones, values, and alarms line up. Verify field names and that the right roles can search the index.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Noise level gauge; time-of-day heat map; location comparison chart.
grandmaExplanation: We watch how your building systems behave. We help you know if something is off with this area: ambient noise level monitoring and trend analysis before harm, damage, or wasted effort piles up.
dataModelAcceleration: Not applicable (no CIM or Operational_Telemetry data model in this SPL).
